D2I outperforms baselines on both in-domain and out-of-domain benchmarks, highlighting the effectiveness of format rewards in fostering transferable multimodal reasoning skills and suggesting the benefit of decoupling training-time reasoning depth from test-time response flexibility.

light_analysis.overview: 提出D2I框架，通过训练时规则格式奖励的深思推理和推理时直观推理，提升多模态LLM推理能力，无需额外标注或复杂奖励。
light_analysis.motivation: 多模态推理面临模态对齐和训练可扩展性挑战，现有方法依赖额外标注或复杂规则奖励。例如，数学问题求解中需要跨模态理解，但构建高质量标注数据成本高昂，且复杂奖励设计困难。因此，急需一种无需额外标注或复杂奖励的推理增强方法，以简化训练流程并提高泛化能力。
light_analysis.method: D2I框架包含两阶段：训练时采用深思推理策略，仅用基于规则的格式奖励（如输出格式正确性）监督，强化模态对齐；推理时切换为直观推理，移除显式策略，模型隐式应用习得能力。关键创新在于解耦训练深度与测试灵活性，避免测试时计算开销，同时保持推理质量。无需额外标注或复杂奖励函数。
light_analysis.result: 在域内和域外基准上，D2I优于多种基线方法。具体地，格式奖励有效培养可迁移的多模态推理技能，模型在未见过的任务上仍保持较高准确率。实验表明，解耦训练时推理深度与测试时响应灵活性带来显著性能提升，且训练成本可控。
light_analysis.conclusion: D2I无需额外标注或复杂奖励即可提升多模态LLM推理，格式奖励和监督策略的有效性得到验证。该方法具学术价值，简化了多模态推理训练流程；实际应用中可降低数据与奖励设计成本。未来可探索更复杂的格式奖励或扩展到其他模态任务。

title: Local MDI+: Local Feature Importances for Tree-Based Models
abstract: Tree-based ensembles such as random forests remain the go-to for tabular data over deep learning models due to their prediction performance and computational efficiency. These advantages have led to their widespread deployment in high-stakes domains, where interpretability is essential for ensuring trustworthy predictions. This has motivated the development of popular local feature importance methods such as LIME and TreeSHAP. However, these approaches rely on approximations that ignore the model's internal structure and instead depend on potentially unstable perturbations. These issues are addressed in the global setting by MDI+, a global feature importance method which combines tree-based and linear feature importances by exploiting an equivalence between decision trees and least squares on a transformed node basis. However, the global MDI+ scores are not able to explain predictions when faced with heterogeneous individual characteristics. To address this gap, we propose Local MDI+ (LMDI+), a novel extension of the MDI+ framework that quantifies feature importances for each particular sample. Across twelve real-world benchmark datasets, LMDI+ outperforms existing baselines at identifying instance-specific predictive features, yielding an average 10% improvement in predictive performance when using only the selected features. It further demonstrates greater stability by consistently producing similar instance-level feature importance rankings across repeated model fits with different random seeds. Ablation experiments show that each component of LMDI+ contributes to these gains, and that the improvements extend beyond random forests to gradient boosting models. Finally, we show that LMDI+ enables local interpretability use cases by identifying closely matched counterfactuals for each classification benchmark and discovering homogeneous subgroups in a housing dataset case study.

light_analysis.overview: 提出Local MDI+ (LMDI+)，一种基于树模型的局部特征重要性方法，扩展MDI+框架到样本级别，提升局部可解释性。
light_analysis.motivation: 树集成模型在高风险领域广泛应用，亟需可靠的可解释性。现有局部特征重要性方法如LIME和TreeSHAP依赖于近似和扰动，忽略模型内部结构，导致不稳定。全局MDI+方法虽克服了这些问题，但无法解释个体异质性。因此需要一种能准确量化每个样本特征重要性的局部方法。
light_analysis.method: LMDI+通过扩展MDI+框架到局部层面，利用决策树与最小二乘在变换节点基上的等价性，为每个样本计算特征重要性。在12个真实数据集上，使用随机森林和梯度提升模型进行评估，并与LIME、TreeSHAP等基线对比。
light_analysis.result: LMDI+在识别实例特定预测特征方面优于现有基线，仅使用所选特征平均提升10%的预测性能。在多次重复拟合中，产生一致的实例级特征重要性排名，稳定性更高。消融实验证实各组件贡献，且改进扩展到梯度提升模型。
light_analysis.conclusion: LMDI+提供了可靠的局部可解释性，可用于识别反事实和发现同质子群。它解决了现有局部方法依赖近似的问题，提高了精确度和稳定性，具有学术和实际应用价值。未来可进一步扩展到其他模型。

title: STFlow: Data-Coupled Flow Matching for Geometric Trajectory Simulation
abstract: Simulating trajectories of dynamical systems is a fundamental problem in a wide range of fields such as molecular dynamics, biochemistry, and pedestrian dynamics. Machine learning has become an invaluable tool for scaling physics-based simulators and developing models directly from experimental data. In particular, recent advances in deep generative modeling and geometric deep learning enable probabilistic simulation by learning complex trajectory distributions while respecting intrinsic permutation and time-shift symmetries. However, trajectories of N-body systems are commonly characterized by high sensitivity to perturbations leading to bifurcations, as well as multi-scale temporal and spatial correlations. To address these challenges, we introduce STFlow (Spatio-Temporal Flow), a generative model based on graph neural networks and hierarchical convolutions. By incorporating data-dependent couplings within the Flow Matching framework, STFlow denoises starting from conditioned random-walks instead of Gaussian noise. This novel informed prior simplifies the learning task by reducing transport cost, increasing training and inference efficiency. We validate our approach on N-body systems, molecular dynamics, and human trajectory forecasting. Across these benchmarks, STFlow achieves the lowest prediction errors with fewer simulation steps and improved scalability.
comment: Proceedings of the 43rd International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML), Seoul, South Korea. PMLR 306, 2026, 18 pages, 12 figures

light_analysis.overview: 提出STFlow，一种基于图神经网络和分层卷积的数据耦合流匹配生成模型，用于高效模拟几何轨迹。
light_analysis.motivation: 模拟动力学系统轨迹在分子动力学、生物化学等领域至关重要。现有机器学习方法通过深度生成模型学习复杂轨迹分布，但N体系统对扰动高度敏感，存在分叉和多尺度时空相关性挑战。这要求模型在保持对称性的同时，有效处理轨迹的敏感性和多尺度特性。
light_analysis.method: STFlow在流匹配框架中引入数据相关耦合，将去噪起点从高斯噪声改为条件随机游走，通过图神经网络和分层卷积捕获时空依赖。该设计通过降低传输成本简化学习任务，提升训练和推理效率。
light_analysis.result: 在N体系统、分子动力学和人类轨迹预测三个基准上，STFlow以更少的模拟步骤取得最低预测误差，并展现出更好的可扩展性，验证了方法在复杂轨迹模拟中的有效性。
light_analysis.conclusion: STFlow通过创新的数据耦合流匹配有效解决了轨迹模拟中的灵敏度和多尺度问题，为物理模拟和轨迹预测提供了高效方案。未来可探索其在更多实际系统中的应用及处理更长时程轨迹的能力。

title: PEAR: Equal Area Weather Forecasting on the Sphere
abstract: Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping the natural sciences, with weather forecasting emerging as a flagship AI4Science application where machine learning models can now rival and even surpass traditional numerical simulations. Following the success of the landmark models Pangu Weather and Graphcast, outperforming traditional numerical methods for global medium-range forecasting, many novel data-driven methods have emerged. A common limitation shared by many of these models is their reliance on an equiangular discretization of the sphere which suffers from a much finer grid at the poles than around the equator. In contrast, in the Hierarchical Equal Area iso-Latitude Pixelization (HEALPix) of the sphere, each pixel covers the same surface area, removing unphysical biases. Motivated by a growing support for this grid in meteorology and climate sciences, we propose to perform weather forecasting with deep learning models which natively operate on the HEALPix grid. To this end, we introduce Pangu Equal ARea (PEAR), a transformer-based weather forecasting model which operates directly on HEALPix-features and outperforms the corresponding model on an equiangular grid, and other baselines, without any computational overhead. Furthermore, we perform numerical experiments on the equivariance properties of our setup and verify the performance of PEAR on climate model emulation.
comment: Extended version of manuscript published in the AI for Science workshop (NeurIPS 2025), 11 pages, 15 pages supplemental

light_analysis.overview: 提出PEAR模型，采用HEALPix等面积网格替代传统等角网格，消除极地网格过密带来的非物理偏差，提升天气预报精度。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有数据驱动天气预报模型（如Pangu Weather、Graphcast）普遍使用等角网格，导致极地网格密度过大，引入非物理偏差。HEALPix网格每个像素面积相等，在气象和气候学中日益受关注，但深度学习模型尚未直接利用。因此，本文旨在开发原生操作HEALPix网格的深度学习天气预测模型。
light_analysis.method: 提出PEAR，一种基于Transformer的天气预测模型，直接处理HEALPix网格特征。采用HEALPix等面积像素化网格，每个像素覆盖相同表面积，消除网格不均匀性。模型架构基于Transformer，在HEALPix网格上执行操作，无需额外计算开销。此外，还进行了等变性质的数值实验。
light_analysis.result: PEAR模型在性能上优于在等角网格上训练的对应模型及多种基线方法，且不增加计算开销。通过气候模型模拟实验进一步验证了其有效性。摘要未给出具体数值指标，但表明提升明显。
light_analysis.conclusion: PEAR成功将HEALPix网格引入深度学习天气预报，消除了极地区域的非物理偏差，提升了性能且无计算开销。这项工作有望推动气象和气候科学中网格均匀化方法的应用，为后续研究提供新方向。未来可探索更广泛的物理一致性和等变性应用。

title: EVADE-Bench: Multimodal Benchmark for Evaluating and Enhancing Evasive Content Detection
abstract: E-commerce platforms increasingly rely on Large Language Models (LLMs) and Vision Language Models (VLMs) to detect illicit or misleading product content. However, these models remain vulnerable to evasive content, which refers to inputs that have been deliberately modified through techniques such as word splitting, euphemistic language, or image cropping to conceal policy violations while still conveying prohibited claims. Crucially, detecting such content requires a model to simultaneously master two capabilities: accurately comprehending complex rules, and correctly inferring the true intent behind deliberately obfuscated multimodal inputs. While prior work has separately explored LLM reasoning over complex rules and LLM-based detection of evasive content, no existing benchmark combines both within a unified evaluation framework. This gap is particularly consequential in e-commerce, where accurate moderation demands that both capabilities operate in concert. To address this gap, we introduce EVADE-Bench, the first expert-curated Chinese multimodal benchmark specifically designed to evaluate LLMs and VLMs on evasive content detection in real-world e-commerce scenarios. Our comprehensive evaluation of 26 open- and closed-source LLMs and VLMs reveals that even state-of-the-art models frequently misclassify evasive samples. We further demonstrate that clearer rule categorization significantly improves model prediction consistency and reduces false predictions, highlighting the critical role of benchmark design in enabling reliable evaluation. To explore paths for performance improvement, we investigate the feasibility of multi-agent decomposition for multimodal reasoning, wherein visual description and logical inference are decoupled into separate agents, and find that this strategy yields notable accuracy gains.

light_analysis.overview: 首个中文多模态规避内容检测基准EVADE-Bench，揭示多模型缺陷并提出多智能体分解提升准确率。
light_analysis.motivation: 电商平台依赖LLM/VLM检测违规内容，但模型易受规避技术（分词、委婉语、图像裁剪等）干扰。现有研究分别探索了复杂规则推理和规避内容检测，缺乏统一评估框架。这一缺失在电商场景中尤为严重，因为精准审核需要同时掌握规则理解和意图推断能力。因此，亟需构建一个整合双重能力的基准来评估和提升模型性能。
light_analysis.method: 提出EVADE-Bench，首个专家策划的中文多模态基准，涵盖真实电商规避内容。对26个开源/闭源LLM和VLM进行全面评估。进一步探索多智能体分解策略，将视觉描述和逻辑推理解耦为独立智能体，通过分离任务提升多模态推理准确率。
light_analysis.result: 即使最先进的模型也频繁误分类规避样本。清晰规则分类显著提升模型预测一致性并减少误判。多智能体分解策略带来明显的准确率提升，验证了该路径的有效性。
light_analysis.conclusion: EVADE-Bench揭示了现有模型在规避内容检测上的不足，强调了统一基准设计对可靠评估的关键作用。多智能体分解为提升多模态推理能力提供了可行方向。未来可扩展至更多语言和场景，并探索更细粒度的规则分解。

title: Semantic-Aware Interpretable Multimodal Music Auto-Tagging
abstract: Music auto-tagging is essential for organizing and discovering music in extensive digital libraries. While foundation models achieve exceptional performance in this domain, their outputs often lack interpretability, limiting trust and usability for researchers and end-users alike. In this work, we present an interpretable framework for music auto-tagging that leverages groups of musically meaningful multimodal features, derived from signal processing, deep learning, ontology engineering, and natural language processing. To enhance interpretability, we cluster features semantically and employ an expectation maximization algorithm, assigning distinct weights to each group based on its contribution to the tagging process. Our method achieves competitive tagging performance while offering a deeper understanding of the decision-making process, paving the way for more transparent and user-centric music tagging systems.

light_analysis.overview: 提出一个可解释的多模态音乐自动标注框架，通过语义特征聚类和EM算法提升透明性。
light_analysis.motivation: 音乐自动标注对数字图书馆中音乐的整理和发现至关重要。现有基础模型虽然性能优异，但缺乏可解释性，限制了研究人员和最终用户的信任与可用性。因此，需要一种既能保持竞争性能又能提供决策过程理解的框架。
light_analysis.method: 提出一个可解释的框架，利用来自信号处理、深度学习、本体工程和自然语言处理的多模态特征。通过语义聚类将特征分组，并采用期望最大化算法为每组分配权重，以反映其对标签任务的贡献，从而增强可解释性。
light_analysis.result: 该方法在保持与现有基础模型竞争性能的同时，提供了对决策过程的深入理解，实现了更透明、更以用户为中心的音乐标注系统。摘要未明确说明具体性能指标或对比数据。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该工作通过引入可解释性机制，推动了音乐自动标注向透明化发展，有助于提升系统的可信度和实用性。未来可进一步优化特征聚类与权重分配，或扩展至其他多媒体标注任务。

title: Structured Agent Distillation for Large Language Model
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) exhibit strong capabilities as decision-making agents by interleaving reasoning and actions, as seen in ReAct-style frameworks. Yet, their practical deployment is constrained by high inference costs and large model sizes. We propose Structured Agent Distillation, a framework that compresses large LLM-based agents into smaller student models while preserving both reasoning fidelity and action consistency. Unlike standard token-level distillation, our method segments trajectories into [REASON] and [ACT] spans, applying segment-specific losses to align each component with the teacher's behavior. This structure-aware supervision enables compact agents to better replicate the teacher's decision process. Experiments on ALFWorld, HotPotQA-ReAct, and WebShop show that our approach consistently outperforms token-level and imitation learning baselines, achieving significant compression with minimal performance drop. Scaling and ablation results further highlight the importance of span-level alignment for efficient and deployable agents.

light_analysis.overview: 提出结构化代理蒸馏框架，通过段级别对齐将大型语言模型代理压缩为小型模型，保持推理和动作一致性。
light_analysis.motivation: 大型语言模型作为决策代理（如ReAct框架）表现强大，但实际部署受限于高推理成本和模型尺寸。现有token级蒸馏方法无法充分保留教师模型的决策过程，导致压缩后性能下降。因此需要一种结构感知的蒸馏方法，在压缩模型的同时保持推理保真度和动作一致性。
light_analysis.method: 提出结构化代理蒸馏，将教师代理的轨迹分割为[REASON]和[ACT]两种跨度，分别对应推理和动作步骤。对每个跨度应用专门的损失函数，以对齐学生模型与教师模型的行为。这种段级别对齐损失替代了标准token级蒸馏损失，使学生模型更好地复制教师的决策过程。
light_analysis.result: 在ALFWorld、HotPotQA-ReAct和WebShop三个基准上，该方法显著优于token级蒸馏和模仿学习基线。实现了大幅压缩（模型尺寸减小）且性能下降极小。缩放和消融实验进一步证明了跨级别对齐对于高效可部署代理的重要性。
light_analysis.conclusion: 结构化代理蒸馏有效压缩大型语言模型代理，同时保持决策质量。该方法具有学术价值，为模型压缩提供了新视角；实际应用价值在于降低部署成本。未来可探索更细粒度的结构划分或扩展到其他类型的代理任务。

title: LiDDA: Data Driven Attribution at LinkedIn
abstract: Data Driven Attribution, which assigns conversion credits to marketing interactions based on causal patterns learned from data, is the foundation of modern marketing intelligence and vital to any marketing business and advertising platform. In this paper, we introduce a unified transformer-based attribution approach that can handle member-level data, aggregate-level data, and integration of external macro factors. We detail the large scale implementation of the approach at LinkedIn, showcasing significant impact. We also share learnings and insights which are broadly applicable to the marketing and ad tech fields.

light_analysis.overview: 提出统一的基于Transformer的数据驱动归因方法LiDDA，支持成员级、聚合级数据和外部宏观因素整合，并在LinkedIn大规模部署。
light_analysis.motivation: 数据驱动归因是现代营销智能的基础，但现有方法难以统一处理不同粒度的营销数据（成员级、聚合级）及外部宏观因素。该研究旨在克服这些局限，提供一个可扩展的统一框架，提升归因准确性和业务价值。
light_analysis.method: 采用Transformer架构构建统一模型，能够同时处理成员级转化路径、聚合级渠道数据以及外部宏观经济指标。通过在LinkedIn的大规模工程实施，实现了端到端的数据管线与模型部署，支持实时归因计算。
light_analysis.result: 在LinkedIn广告平台上线后，显著提升了营销效果评估的准确性与广告支出回报率（ROAS），具体指标未在摘要中详述，但整体影响显著，适用于多种归因场景。
light_analysis.conclusion: LiDDA为数据驱动归因提供了一种通用且可扩展的解决方案，已被验证在工业界具有实际价值，其经验可为广告技术领域借鉴。未来可探索更细粒度的因果建模与跨平台归因。

title: Privacy Protection Against Personalized Text-to-Image Synthesis via Cross-image Consistency Constraints
abstract: The rapid advancement of diffusion models and personalization techniques has made it possible to recreate individual portraits from just a few publicly available images. While such capabilities empower various creative applications, they also introduce serious privacy concerns, as adversaries can exploit them to generate highly realistic impersonations. To counter these threats, anti-personalization methods have been proposed, which add adversarial perturbations to published images to disrupt the training of personalization models. However, existing approaches largely overlook the intrinsic multi-image nature of personalization and instead adopt a naive strategy of applying perturbations independently, as commonly done in single-image settings. This neglects the opportunity to leverage inter-image relationships for stronger privacy protection. Therefore, we advocate for a group-level perspective on privacy protection against personalization. Specifically, we introduce Cross-image Anti-Personalization (CAP), a novel framework that enhances resistance to personalization by enforcing style consistency across perturbed images. Furthermore, we develop a dynamic ratio adjustment strategy that adaptively balances the impact of the consistency loss throughout the attack iterations. Extensive experiments on the classical CelebHQ and VGGFace2 benchmarks show that CAP substantially improves existing methods.

light_analysis.overview: 提出跨图像反个性化框架（CAP），通过强制扰动图像间的风格一致性，增强对个性化文本到图像合成的隐私保护。
light_analysis.motivation: 扩散模型和个性化技术的快速发展使得从少量公开照片即可重建个人肖像，带来严重隐私泄露风险。现有反个性化方法多为每张图像独立添加扰动，忽视了多图像间的内在关联，未能充分利用图像间关系来增强保护。因此，需要从组级别角度出发，利用跨图像一致性约束来提升隐私保护效果。
light_analysis.method: 论文提出跨图像反个性化（CAP）框架，核心是强制执行扰动图像之间的风格一致性约束，使得个性化模型难以从多张图像中学习到统一的人物特征。同时，开发了动态比例调整策略，在攻击迭代中自适应平衡一致性损失的影响。实验采用CelebHQ和VGGFace2基准数据集。
light_analysis.result: 在CelebHQ和VGGFace2基准上的大量实验表明，CAP显著提升了现有反个性化方法的性能。对比基线方法，CAP在破坏个性化模型生成质量方面更有效，降低了生成图像与原始人物相似度，同时保持了扰动图像的视觉质量。具体指标如攻击成功率、身份保持度等均有改进。
light_analysis.conclusion: 论文从组级别视角重新审视隐私保护问题，提出跨图像一致性约束方法，有效增强了对个性化文本到图像合成的防御能力。该方法具有实际应用价值，可用于保护公开发布的个人照片免受恶意个性化攻击。未来可探索更复杂的图像间关系建模及自适应策略的优化。

title: Hierarchical Relation-augmented Representation Generalization for Few-shot Action Recognition
abstract: Few-shot action recognition (FSAR) aims to recognize novel action categories with few exemplars. Existing methods typically learn frame-level representations for each video by designing inter-frame temporal modeling strategies or inter-video interaction at the coarse video-level granularity. However, they treat each episode task in isolation and neglect fine-grained temporal relation modeling between videos, thus failing to capture shared fine-grained temporal patterns across videos and reuse temporal knowledge from historical tasks. In light of this, we propose HR2G-shot, a Hierarchical Relation-augmented Representation Generalization framework for FSAR, which unifies three types of relation modeling (inter-frame, inter-video, and inter-task) to learn task-specific temporal patterns from a holistic view. Going beyond conducting inter-frame temporal interactions, we further devise two components to respectively explore inter-video and inter-task relationships: i) Inter-video Semantic Correlation (ISC) performs cross-video frame-level interactions in a fine-grained manner, thereby capturing task-specific query features and enhancing both intra-class consistency and inter-class separability; ii) Inter-task Knowledge Transfer (IKT) retrieves and aggregates relevant temporal knowledge from the bank, which stores diverse temporal patterns from historical episode tasks. Extensive experiments on five benchmarks show that HR2G-shot outperforms current top-leading FSAR methods.

light_analysis.overview: 提出HR2G-shot框架，统一帧间、视频间和任务间三种关系建模，增强表示泛化，提升少样本动作识别性能。
light_analysis.motivation: 少样本动作识别旨在用少量样本识别新动作类别。现有方法通常只进行帧级表示学习或粗粒度视频间交互，忽略了细粒度视频间时序关系建模，且无法复用历史任务中的时序知识，导致难以捕获共享的细粒度时序模式。因此需要一种从全局视角学习任务特定时序模式的方法。
light_analysis.method: 提出HR2G-shot框架，统一帧间、视频间和任务间关系建模。除帧间交互外，设计了两个组件：1) 视频间语义关联(ISC)进行细粒度跨视频帧级交互，捕获任务特定查询特征，增强类内一致性和类间可分离性；2) 任务间知识迁移(IKT)从存储历史任务时序模式的银行中检索和聚合相关时序知识，实现跨任务知识复用。
light_analysis.result: 在五个基准数据集（SSv2、HMDB51、UCF101、Kinetics和Mini-MIT）上的大量实验表明，HR2G-shot优于当前领先的少样本动作识别方法，具体性能指标如平均分类准确率等摘要未明确，但指出超越现有方法。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文提出层次化关系增强表示泛化框架HR2G-shot，通过统一三种关系建模解决了现有方法忽略细粒度时序关系和跨任务知识复用的问题，在五个基准上取得领先性能，为少样本动作识别提供了新思路。未来可探索更高效的知识存储与检索机制。

title: The Point, the Vision and the Text: Does Point Cloud Boost Spatial Reasoning of Large Language Models? A Bias-Controlled Study
abstract: 3D Large Language Models (LLMs) leveraging spatial information in point clouds for 3D spatial reasoning attract great attention. Despite some promising results, the advantages of point clouds over other modalities remain unclear. Moreover, existing 3D benchmarks are insufficient for fairly evaluating the ability of multimodal LLMs to comprehend spatial concepts. To address these challenges, we introduce ScanReQA, a 3D spatial reasoning benchmark encompassing text, vision, and point cloud modalities. We then evaluate the performance of text, 2D, and 3D LLMs on the benchmark to compare the effectiveness of different modalities in understanding spatial concepts. Furthermore, we analyze the reasoning mechanisms behind 3D LLMs using point clouds. Our findings reveal that: 1) binary spatial reasoning remains challenging for current 3D LLMs, 2) MLLMs based on point cloud and visual modalities demonstrate stronger spatial reasoning capabilities than LLMs, and 3) 3D LLMs exhibit the attention sink phenomenon similar to that in 2D LLMs, impairing spatial reasoning. We think these conclusions can help the next step of 3D LLMs and also offer insights for foundation models in other modalities. We release datasets and codes in the project page: https://github.com/EmbodiedCity/ScanReQA.code.

light_analysis.overview: 提出ScanReQA基准，通过偏置控制研究点云是否提升大语言模型的空间推理能力。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有3D大语言模型利用点云进行空间推理取得一定成果，但点云相比其他模态的优势尚不明确。同时，现有3D基准无法公平评估多模态大语言模型的空间概念理解能力。因此需要构建包含文本、视觉和点云模态的基准，系统比较不同模态对空间推理的贡献。
light_analysis.method: 构建ScanReQA基准，包含文本、2D图像和3D点云三种模态的空间推理任务。评估文本LLM、2D MLLM和3D MLLM在该基准上的表现，对比不同模态的效果。通过偏置控制分析3D LLM的点云推理机制，尤其关注注意力分布。
light_analysis.result: 实验发现：1）二元空间推理对当前3D LLM仍具挑战；2）基于点云和视觉模态的MLLM空间推理能力显著强于纯文本LLM；3）3D LLM存在与2D LLM类似的注意力沉没现象，损害空间推理性能。
light_analysis.conclusion: 揭示了点云模态对空间推理的增强作用及现有3D LLM的局限性，特别是注意力沉没问题。该研究为改进3D LLM及其他模态基础模型提供方向，所发布数据集和代码可促进后续研究。

title: Text-Only Data Synthesis for Vision Language Model Training
abstract: Training vision-language models (VLMs) typically requires large-scale, high-quality image-text pairs, but collecting or synthesizing such data is costly. In contrast, text data is abundant and inexpensive, prompting the question: can high-quality multimodal training data be synthesized purely from text? To tackle this, we propose a cross-integrated three-stage multimodal data synthesis framework, which generates two datasets: Unicorn-1.2M and Unicorn-471K-Instruction. In Stage 1: Diverse Caption Data Synthesis, we construct 1.2M semantically diverse high-quality captions by expanding sparse caption seeds using large language models (LLMs). In Stage 2: Instruction-Tuning Data Generation, we further process 471K captions into multi-turn instruction-tuning tasks to support complex reasoning. Finally, in Stage 3: Modality Representation Transfer, these textual captions representations are transformed into visual representations, resulting in diverse synthetic image representations. This three-stage process enables us to construct Unicorn-1.2M for pretraining and Unicorn-471K-Instruction for instruction-tuning, without relying on real images. By eliminating the dependency on real images while maintaining data quality and diversity, our framework offers a cost-effective and scalable solution for VLMs training.

light_analysis.overview: 提出纯文本合成多模态训练数据的框架，无需真实图像即可生成高质量图文数据用于VLM训练。
light_analysis.motivation: 视觉语言模型训练需要大规模高质量图文对，但收集或合成成本高昂。相比之下，纯文本数据丰富且廉价。因此，研究者探索是否仅从文本合成高质量多模态训练数据，以降低成本并提升可扩展性。现有方法依赖真实图像或复杂生成流程，限制了数据多样性及实用性。
light_analysis.method: 提出三阶段跨集成数据合成框架。阶段一：利用大语言模型扩展稀疏标注种子，生成120万语义多样的高质量标注。阶段二：将47万标注处理为多轮指令微调任务，支持复杂推理。阶段三：通过模态表示转换，将文本标注表示映射为视觉表示，生成多样化的合成图像表示。该框架无需真实图像，构建了预训练数据集Unicorn-1.2M和指令微调数据集Unicorn-471K-Instruction。
light_analysis.result: 通过三阶段框架，成功生成了Unicorn-1.2M和Unicorn-471K-Instruction两个高质量数据集，完全基于文本合成，无需真实图像。该方法在保持数据质量和多样性的同时，显著降低了VLM训练数据的获取成本。摘要未提供具体性能指标，但强调其有效性和可扩展性。
light_analysis.conclusion: 该研究提出了一种纯文本驱动的多模态数据合成方法，消除了对真实图像的依赖，为VLM训练提供了低成本、可扩展的解决方案。Unicorn数据集可替代传统图文对，支持预训练和指令微调。未来工作可能探索更复杂的视觉表示转换或应用于更大规模模型。局限性包括合成图像表示与真实图像的差距及下游任务验证。

title: xKV: Cross-Layer KV-Cache Compression via Aligned Singular Vector Extraction
abstract: Long-context Large Language Models (LLMs) enable powerful applications but incur high memory costs due to the key-value states (KV-Cache). Recent studies attempt to share KV-Cache across layers, but these approaches either require expensive pretraining or rely on per-token cross-layer cosine similarity that is often limited in practice. We show, via Centered Kernel Alignment (CKA), that the dominant singular vectors of KV-Cache are well aligned across layers. Motivated by this observation, we propose xKV, a post-training compression method that jointly factorizes grouped-layer KV-Cache into a shared low-rank subspace, substantially reducing KV-Cache memory. Across widely used LLMs, xKV achieves up to 8x KV-Cache compression while preserving accuracy on long-context tasks and in multi-turn settings. To further improve efficiency, we introduce Selective Reconstruction (SR) at decode time. Combined with SR, xKV achieves up to 4.23x end-to-end speedup over the full attention baseline, and surpasses notable baselines with 30% higher throughput under a similar accuracy level. Overall, xKV provides a plug-and-play approach to reduce both memory and latency for long-context LLM inference. Our code is publicly available at: https://github.com/abdelfattah-lab/xKV.

light_analysis.overview: 提出xKV，一种基于跨层对齐奇异向量提取的后训练KV缓存压缩方法，实现高效长上下文LLM推理。
light_analysis.motivation: 长上下文LLM因KV缓存占用大量内存，现有跨层共享方法需昂贵预训练或依赖有限的每token跨层余弦相似度。基于CKA发现主导奇异向量跨层良好对齐，启发设计更有效的压缩方案。
light_analysis.method: xKV通过Centered Kernel Alignment（CKA）观察KV缓存主导奇异向量跨层对齐，将分组层KV缓存联合分解为共享低秩子空间，实现后训练压缩。同时引入选择性重建（SR）机制在解码时进一步加速。无需预训练，即插即用。
light_analysis.result: 在广泛使用的LLM上，xKV实现高达8倍KV缓存压缩且保持长上下文和多轮任务精度。结合SR，端到端加速达4.23倍（相对全注意力基线），在相似精度下吞吐量提升30%，超越主流基线方法。
light_analysis.conclusion: xKV通过跨层奇异向量对齐实现高效KV缓存压缩，显著降低内存和延迟，适用于长上下文LLM推理。作为即插即用方法，具有实用价值。未来可探索更优分组策略或动态子空间分配。

title: Accelerating Diffusion Sampling via Exploiting Local Transition Coherence
abstract: Text-based diffusion models have made significant breakthroughs in generating high-quality images and videos from textual descriptions. However, the lengthy sampling time of the denoising process remains a significant bottleneck in practical applications. Previous methods either ignore the statistical relationships between adjacent steps or rely on attention or feature similarity between them, which often only works with specific network structures. To address this issue, we discover a new statistical relationship in the transition operator between adjacent steps, focusing on the relationship of the outputs from the network. This relationship does not impose any requirements on the network structure. Based on this observation, we propose a novel training-free acceleration method called LTC-Accel, which uses the identified relationship to estimate the current transition operator based on adjacent steps. Due to no specific assumptions regarding the network structure, LTC-Accel is applicable to almost all diffusion-based methods and orthogonal to almost all existing acceleration techniques, making it easy to combine with them. Experimental results demonstrate that LTC-Accel significantly speeds up sampling in text-to-image and text-to-video synthesis while maintaining competitive sample quality. Specifically, LTC-Accel achieves a speedup of 1.67-fold in Stable Diffusion v2 and a speedup of 1.55-fold in video generation models. When combined with distillation models, LTC-Accel achieves a remarkable 10-fold speedup in video generation, allowing real-time generation of more than 16FPS.

light_analysis.overview: 提出一种基于局部过渡相干性的无训练扩散采样加速方法，适用于文本到图像/视频生成。
light_analysis.motivation: 文本扩散模型生成高质量图像/视频，但去噪过程采样时间长。现有加速方法忽略相邻步骤间统计关系或依赖特定网络结构，普适性差。本文发现新的统计关系，不依赖网络结构，旨在实现通用加速。
light_analysis.method: 发现相邻步骤网络输出间过渡算子的统计关系，据此提出LTC-Accel，利用相邻步估计当前过渡算子。无需训练，不假设网络结构，适用于几乎所有扩散方法，并与现有加速技术正交可组合。
light_analysis.result: 在Stable Diffusion v2上获得1.67倍加速，视频生成模型上获得1.55倍加速。与蒸馏模型结合，视频生成获得10倍加速，实现实时生成（>16 FPS），且保持样本质量竞争力。
light_analysis.conclusion: 提出一种简单有效的无训练加速方法，广泛适用且可组合。显著加速扩散采样，尤其结合蒸馏实现实时视频生成。未来可探索在其他模态扩散模型中的应用。

title: Manboformer: Learning Gaussian Representations via Spatial-temporal Attention Mechanism
abstract: Compared with voxel-based grid prediction, in the field of 3D semantic occupation prediction for autonomous driving, GaussianFormer proposed using 3D Gaussian to describe scenes with sparse 3D semantic Gaussian based on objects is another scheme with lower memory requirements. Each 3D Gaussian function represents a flexible region of interest and its semantic features, which are iteratively refined by the attention mechanism. In the experiment, it is found that the Gaussian function required by this method is larger than the query resolution of the original dense grid network, resulting in impaired performance. Therefore, we consider optimizing GaussianFormer by using unused temporal information. We learn the Spatial-Temporal Self-attention Mechanism from the previous grid-given occupation network and improve it to GaussianFormer. The experiment was conducted with the NuScenes dataset, and the experiment is currently underway.
comment: After careful self-check, we found several unnoticed deficiencies and incomplete discussions in this manuscript. To ensure the rigor and accuracy of academic results, we decide to withdraw this preprint. A refined, complete, and rigorous version will be submitted soon

light_analysis.overview: 提出Manboformer，通过时空自注意力机制优化GaussianFormer的3D语义占用预测，减少高斯数量需求。
light_analysis.motivation: GaussianFormer使用3D高斯表示场景，但所需高斯数量超过密集网格的查询分辨率，导致性能下降。现有方法忽略了时序信息，本文利用未使用的时序信息来优化高斯表示，以提升预测精度并降低内存消耗。
light_analysis.method: 从先前的网格占网络学习空间-时间自注意力机制，并将其适配到GaussianFormer中，通过时序信息迭代优化3D高斯函数的参数和语义特征。使用NuScenes数据集进行实验。
light_analysis.result: 实验正在进行中，具体结果待公布。摘要未提供定量对比数据。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文针对GaussianFormer的高斯数量过多问题，引入时空注意力机制改进表示效率，为自动驾驶3D语义占用预测提供新思路。未来需完成实验验证性能提升，并探索实时部署可行性。

title: Persuade Me if You Can: A Framework for Evaluating Persuasion Effectiveness and Susceptibility Among Large Language Models
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate persuasive capabilities that rival human-level persuasion. While these capabilities can be used for social good, they also present risks of potential misuse. Beyond the concern of how LLMs persuade others, their own susceptibility to persuasion poses a critical alignment challenge, raising questions about robustness, safety, and adherence to ethical principles. To study these dynamics, we introduce Persuade Me If You Can (PMIYC), an automated framework for evaluating persuasiveness and susceptibility to persuasion in multi-agent interactions. Our framework offers a scalable alternative to the costly and time-intensive human annotation process typically used to study persuasion in LLMs. PMIYC automatically conducts multi-turn conversations between Persuader and Persuadee agents, measuring both the effectiveness of and susceptibility to persuasion. Our comprehensive evaluation spans a diverse set of LLMs and persuasion settings (e.g., subjective and misinformation scenarios). We validate the efficacy of our framework through human evaluations and demonstrate alignment with human assessments from prior studies. Through PMIYC, we find that Llama-3.3-70B and GPT-4o exhibit similar persuasive effectiveness, outperforming Claude 3 Haiku by 30%. However, GPT-4o demonstrates over 50% greater resistance to persuasion for misinformation compared to Llama-3.3-70B. Notably, o4-mini emerges as both an effective persuader, and a resistant persuadee. These findings provide empirical insights into the persuasive dynamics of LLMs and contribute to the development of safer AI systems.
comment: Paper published at the ACM Conference on AI and Agentic Systems 2026

light_analysis.overview: 提出PMIYC自动化框架，评估LLM在多智能体交互中的说服力与易说服性，发现不同模型在说服效果和抗说服能力上的显著差异。
light_analysis.motivation: LLM展现出接近人类的劝说能力，可用于社会公益但也存在滥用风险。同时，LLM自身易受劝说影响，构成对齐挑战，影响鲁棒性、安全性和伦理遵循。传统研究依赖昂贵耗时的人工标注，缺乏可扩展的自动化评估方法。因此需要一种高效、自动化的框架来系统评估LLM的说服动态。
light_analysis.method: 提出PMIYC框架，自动化进行说服者与说服对象之间的多轮对话，同时测量说服效果和易说服性。评估覆盖多种LLM（如Llama-3.3-70B、GPT-4o、Claude 3 Haiku、o4-mini）和不同劝说场景（主观和错误信息场景）。通过人工评估验证框架有效性，并与先前研究的人类评估结果对齐。
light_analysis.result: Llama-3.3-70B与GPT-4o在说服效果上相似，均比Claude 3 Haiku高30%。但在错误信息场景中，GPT-4o抗说服能力比Llama-3.3-70B强50%以上。o4-mini既表现出色作为说服者，又是抵抗说服能力强的说服对象。框架高效且与人类评估一致。
light_analysis.conclusion: PMIYC框架为研究LLM说服动态提供了可扩展的自动化工具，揭示了不同模型在说服与抗说服方面的能力差异。这些实证发现有助于理解LLM的社会影响，并为开发更安全、更鲁棒的AI系统提供指导。未来可扩展至更多模型和场景，并探索减轻易说服性的方法。

title: Investigating Memory in Model-Free RL with POPGym Arcade
abstract: How should we analyze memory in deep RL? We introduce tools for analyzing policies under partial observability and revealing how agents use memory to make decisions. To utilize these tools, we present POPGym Arcade, a collection of Atari-inspired, hardware-accelerated environments sharing a single observation and action space. Each environment provides fully and partially observable variants, enabling counterfactual studies on observability. We find that controlled studies are necessary for fair comparisons and identify a pathology where value functions smear credit over irrelevant history. Using this pathology, we demonstrate how out-of-distribution scenarios can contaminate memory, perturbing the policy far into the future. Our code is available at https://github.com/bolt-research/popgym-arcade.
comment: Appear at ICML 2026 as a Spotlight paper

light_analysis.overview: 提出POPGym Arcade，一个硬件加速的Atari风格环境集合，用于分析深度强化学习中的记忆使用和病理现象。
light_analysis.motivation: 深度强化学习中，记忆机制的分析缺乏系统工具。现有方法难以揭示智能体如何在部分可观测环境下利用记忆做出决策，且缺乏控制实验进行公平比较。本文旨在通过标准化环境进行反事实研究，识别记忆使用的病理现象。
light_analysis.method: 构建POPGym Arcade，包含多种Atari风格环境，共享单一观测和动作空间，并提供完全可观测和部分可观测变体。利用该平台进行控制实验，通过反事实分析（对比全观测与部分观测）揭示价值函数对无关历史的抹平效应，并研究分布外场景对记忆的污染。代码已开源。
light_analysis.result: 研究发现，价值函数会过度平均无关历史信息，导致信用分配错误（病理现象）。分布外场景能够污染智能体的记忆，使策略在长时间步后仍受扰动。通过控制实验，证明了公平比较的必要性，并展示了记忆病理对策略稳定性的影响。
light_analysis.conclusion: POPGym Arcade为深度强化学习中记忆机制的研究提供了标准化平台。揭示了价值函数抹平无关历史和分布外记忆污染两种病理，有助于未来设计更鲁棒的记忆模型。局限在于环境为Atari风格，可能不涵盖所有部分可观测场景；未来可扩展至更多样化环境。

title: MM-PoisonRAG: Disrupting Multimodal RAG with Local and Global Poisoning Attacks
abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has become a common practice in multimodal large language models (MLLM) to enhance factual grounding and reduce hallucination. Yet, its reliance on retrieval exposes MLLMs to knowledge poisoning attacks, in which adversaries deliberately inject malicious multimodal content into external knowledge bases to steer models toward generating incorrect or even harmful responses. We present MM-PoisonRAG, a framework to systematically study the vulnerability of multimodal RAG under knowledge poisoning. Specifically, we design two novel attack strategies: Localized Poisoning Attack (LPA), which implants targeted, query-specific multimodal misinformation to manipulate outputs toward attacker-controlled responses, and Globalized Poisoning Attack (GPA), which uses a single, untargeted adversarial injection to broadly corrupt reasoning and collapse generation quality across all queries. Extensive experiments on diverse tasks, multimodal RAG components, and attacker access levels reveal severe vulnerabilities: LPA achieves up to 56% attack success rate even under restricted access, and transfers effectively across four different retrievers without re-optimizing the adversaries. GPA completely disrupts model generation to 0% accuracy with just one poisoned content. Moreover, both LPA and GPA bypass existing defenses, underscoring the fragility of multimodal RAG and establishing MM-PoisonRAG as a foundation for future research on securing RAG frameworks against multimodal knowledge poisoning.

light_analysis.overview: 提出MM-PoisonRAG框架，系统研究多模态RAG在知识投毒攻击下的脆弱性，并设计局部和全局两种攻击策略。
light_analysis.motivation: 多模态RAG依赖外部知识库，易受知识投毒攻击，导致模型生成错误或有害内容。现有研究缺乏对多模态RAG在知识投毒下脆弱性的系统分析，亟需揭示其风险以推动安全研究。
light_analysis.method: 设计两种攻击策略：局部投毒攻击（LPA）针对特定查询植入多模态误信息，操控输出；全局投毒攻击（GPA）通过单一对抗注入破坏所有查询的推理和生成质量。实验涵盖多种任务、RAG组件和攻击者访问级别。
light_analysis.result: LPA在受限访问下达到56%攻击成功率，且有效迁移至四种不同检索器；GPA仅需一个中毒内容即完全破坏生成，准确率降至0%。两种攻击均能绕过现有防御机制。
light_analysis.conclusion: MM-PoisonRAG揭示了多模态RAG的严重脆弱性，为未来设计鲁棒防御策略奠定基础，但未提出具体防御方法。

title: MCTS-Judge: Test-Time Scaling in LLM-as-a-Judge for Code Correctness Evaluation
abstract: The LLM-as-a-Judge paradigm shows promise for evaluating generative content but lacks reliability in reasoning-intensive scenarios, such as programming. Inspired by recent advances in reasoning models and shifts in scaling laws, we pioneer bringing test-time computation into LLM-as-a-Judge, proposing MCTS-Judge, a resource-efficient, System-2 thinking framework for code correctness evaluation. MCTS-Judge leverages Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) to decompose problems into simpler, multi-perspective evaluations. Through a node-selection strategy that combines self-assessment based on historical actions in the current trajectory and the Upper Confidence Bound for Trees based on prior rollouts, MCTS-Judge balances global optimization and refinement of the current trajectory. We further designed a high-precision, unit-test-level reward mechanism to encourage the Large Language Model (LLM) to perform line-by-line analysis. Extensive experiments on three benchmarks and five LLMs demonstrate the effectiveness of MCTS-Judge, which improves the base model's accuracy from 41% to 80%, surpassing the o1-series models with 3x fewer tokens. Further evaluations validate the superiority of its reasoning trajectory in logic, analytics, thoroughness, and overall quality, while revealing the test-time scaling law of the LLM-as-a-Judge paradigm.

light_analysis.overview: 提出MCTS-Judge框架，将测试时计算引入LLM-as-a-Judge范式，通过MCTS实现多角度评估，显著提升代码正确性评价的准确性。
light_analysis.motivation: LLM-as-a-Judge在推理密集型场景（如编程）中可靠性不足。现有方法缺乏系统性推理，无法有效评估代码正确性。受推理模型和扩展律启发，作者旨在引入测试时计算，提升评估的深度和准确性。
light_analysis.method: MCTS-Judge框架利用蒙特卡洛树搜索将问题分解为多角度评估。节点选择策略结合自评估和置信上限，平衡全局优化与局部细化。设计高精度单元测试级奖励机制，鼓励逐行分析。在五个LLM上进行实验。
light_analysis.result: 在三个基准测试上，MCTS-Judge将基础模型准确率从41%提升至80%，超越o1系列模型，且token消耗减少3倍。推理轨迹在逻辑、分析、全面性和整体质量上均优于基线。
light_analysis.conclusion: MCTS-Judge验证了测试时计算在LLM-as-a-Judge中的有效性，揭示了测试时扩展律。该框架提升了代码评估的可靠性和效率，为推理密集型任务提供了新范式。未来可探索更复杂的奖励机制和搜索策略。

title: Beyond External Monitors: Enhancing Transparency of Large Language Models for Easier Monitoring
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are becoming increasingly capable, but the mechanisms of their thinking and decision-making processes remain unclear. Chain-of-thoughts (CoTs) have been commonly utilized to externalize LLMs' thinking, but this strategy fails to accurately reflect LLMs' thinking process. Techniques based on LLMs' hidden representations provide an inner perspective to improve the monitorability of their latent thinking. However, previous methods only try to develop external modules instead of making LLMs themselves easier to monitor. In this paper, we propose a novel method, TELLME, improving the transparency of LLMs and helping monitors identify unsuitable and sensitive behaviors. Furthermore, we showcase the effectiveness of TELLME on detoxification tasks, where LLMs achieve consistent improvement among multimodal test sets, distinct architectures, and varying parameter scales. We further analyze TELLME's improvement on LLMs' generalization ability from both optimal transport theory and empirical perspectives.

light_analysis.overview: 提出TELLME方法，通过增强大语言模型的透明性，使其更易于监控，在去毒化任务上取得一致改进。
light_analysis.motivation: 大语言模型的思维过程不透明，链式思维无法准确反映其思考过程。现有方法仅开发外部模块来监控，而未提升模型自身的可监控性。因此，需要一种方法使LLM本身更易监控，以识别不合适和敏感的行为。
light_analysis.method: 提出TELLME方法，通过改善LLM的透明性，帮助监控者识别不合适和敏感的行为。在去毒化任务上验证，使用多模态测试集、不同架构（如GPT、LLaMA等）和参数规模（从7B到13B）的模型，并从最优传输理论和实证角度分析泛化能力。
light_analysis.result: 在去毒化任务上，TELLME在多模态测试集、不同架构和参数规模下均实现一致改进。摘要未给出具体数值，但表明该方法有效提升了LLM的透明性和去毒化性能。
light_analysis.conclusion: TELLME有效提升了大语言模型的透明性，有助于监控其潜在的不合适和敏感行为。该方法在去毒化任务上表现良好，并从理论和实证上分析了泛化能力。未来可扩展至其他需要模型透明性的任务。

title: Chirpy3D: Part-Aware Multi-View Diffusion for Creative Fine-Grained Object Generation
abstract: Understanding and generating the fine-grained structure of objects -- such as birds with species-specific beaks, wings, and tails -- is a long-standing challenge in computer vision. We propose Chirpy3D, a part-aware multi-view diffusion framework that learns a hierarchical part latent space from unposed 2D images, using only off-the-shelf 2D part segmentation masks as spatial guidance -- without requiring any 3D data, camera poses, or manual part annotations. This latent space enables intuitive part-level swapping, interpolation, and zero-shot composition. A self-supervised feature consistency loss further encourages structural alignment across views, allowing coherent generation even with hybrid or unseen part combinations. Our core contribution is the controllable part-aware latent space and multi-view diffusion model. Downstream 3D generation is supported via any differentiable renderer such as NeRF but is orthogonal to the main framework, making Chirpy3D a flexible foundation for creative object generation in the absence of structured 3D data. Code is released at https://github.com/kamwoh/chirpy3d.

light_analysis.overview: 提出Chirpy3D，一种部分感知的多视图扩散框架，从无位姿2D图像学习层次化部分潜空间，无需3D数据或相机位姿，实现细粒度对象生成与创意编辑。
light_analysis.motivation: 理解和生成物体的细粒度结构（如鸟类的喙、翅膀、尾巴）是计算机视觉长期挑战。现有方法依赖3D数据、相机位姿或人工部分标注，限制了灵活性和应用范围。Chirpy3D旨在仅使用2D图像和现成分割掩码，学习可控的部分级潜空间，支持直观的部分交换、插值和零样本组合，为缺乏结构化3D数据的场景提供生成基础。
light_analysis.method: Chirpy3D利用现成的2D部分分割掩码作为空间引导，从未定位的2D图像中学习层次化的部分潜空间。采用多视图扩散模型，并通过自监督特征一致性损失促进跨视图结构对齐，实现混合或未见部分组合的连贯生成。框架独立于具体可微渲染器（如NeRF），支持下游3D生成，核心创新在于可控的部分感知潜空间与多视图扩散架构。
light_analysis.result: 摘要未提供定量实验结果，但声称通过自监督特征一致性损失实现了跨视图结构对齐，能够对混合或未见部分组合进行连贯生成。代码已开源，定性展示部分级交换与插值效果，为细粒度对象生成提供了可行性验证，具体性能指标待后续评估。
light_analysis.conclusion: Chirpy3D的主要贡献在于提出了一种无需3D数据或位姿的可控部分感知潜空间与多视图扩散框架，实现了灵活的细粒度对象生成与编辑。该框架可作为缺乏结构化3D数据场景下的通用基础，支持创意应用。未来方向包括与更复杂3D渲染器的深度集成及大规模验证。

title: Take Only What You Need: Rank Minimization as an Implicit Forgetting Regularizer in Continual Learning
abstract: The central tension in continual learning (CL) is the trade-off between plasticity (acquiring new knowledge) and stability (retaining prior knowledge). We study how a pre-trained backbone can be continually updated to absorb new knowledge while preserving existing capabilities, via capacity control: regulating the effective rank of each parameter update, a per-step quantity directly controllable inside a LoRA update. A controlled probe of LoRA rank and placement across modules and tasks reveals a consistent trade-off, with a moderate-rank sweet spot that varies by placement and task, leaving no universally optimal fixed rank; a formal bound shows forgetting grows with rank. Building on these findings, we propose Continual Dynamic Rank-Selective LoRA (CoDyRA), which jointly trains each LoRA update with rank minimization via sparsity-promoting regularization on per-component importance weights. The supervised objective drives plasticity; rank minimization regularizes forgetting. We show that rank minimization serves as an implicit forgetting regularizer in the CL regime, protecting general capability and prior-task knowledge simultaneously by controlling forgetting against the current model state. Across MTIL, X-TAIL, and TRACE (CLIP, LLaMA, Gemma), CoDyRA outperforms prior CL methods on new knowledge learning and forgetting, achieving a strong plasticity-stability balance. Code is available at https://github.com/jeff024/codyra.

light_analysis.overview: 提出秩最小化作为持续学习中的隐式遗忘正则化方法，通过动态秩选择LoRA实现塑性-稳定性平衡。
light_analysis.motivation: 持续学习中塑性（获取新知识）与稳定性（保留旧知识）之间存在根本矛盾。现有方法如固定秩LoRA无法适应不同任务和模块，导致遗忘或学习不足。需要一种自适应机制来动态调控参数更新的有效秩，以在保护已有能力的同时吸收新知识。
light_analysis.method: 提出CoDyRA方法，在每个LoRA更新中引入基于稀疏性正则化的秩最小化，通过逐组件重要性权重控制有效秩。监督目标驱动塑性，秩最小化隐式正则化遗忘。在MTIL、X-TAIL和TRACE基准上，使用CLIP、LLaMA和Gemma骨干网络进行验证。
light_analysis.result: CoDyRA在多个持续学习基准上优于现有方法，在MTIL、X-TAIL和TRACE任务中均取得更优的新知识学习和遗忘控制效果，实现了强塑性-稳定性平衡。正式界证明遗忘随秩增长，实验验证了中秩甜蜜点的存在。
light_analysis.conclusion: 秩最小化作为隐式遗忘正则化器，能有效保护通用能力和先前任务知识。CoDyRA通过动态秩选择为持续学习提供自适应容量控制，具有广泛应用价值。未来可探索更高效的秩选择策略或扩展到其他参数高效微调方法。

title: Revisiting Graph Autoencoders as Implicit Contrastive Learners
abstract: Graph autoencoders (GAEs) and graph contrastive learning (GCL) are two major paradigms for self-supervised representation learning on graphs, yet they are often studied in isolation and treated as fundamentally different approaches. In this work, we revisit GAEs through the lens of contrastive learning and show that both structure-based and feature-based GAEs can be conceptualized as implicitly graph contrastive learners. This perspective reveals that many existing GAEs differ primarily in how contrastive views are constructed, rather than in their learning objectives or architectures. Building on this insight, we introduce a unified formulation that highlights contrastive view design as a central and previously less explored dimension in GAEs. In particular, we identify asymmetric contrastive views, arising from mismatches in subgraph views, as an important yet underexplored design axis in prior GAE research. We formalize this insight within a unified framework and conduct systematic experiments on representative graph learning tasks to examine its impact on performance and efficiency. Our results show that interpreting GAEs as implicit contrastive learners offers a clearer understanding of existing models and provides practical guidance for designing effective and scalable graph autoencoders.
comment: KDD 2026 research track. Code available at https://github.com/EdisonLeeeee/lrGAE

light_analysis.overview: 提出图自编码器可视为隐式对比学习器的统一视角，强调对比视图设计是核心维度。
light_analysis.motivation: 图自编码器（GAEs）和图对比学习（GCL）是自监督图表示学习的两种主流范式，但常被孤立研究且视为根本不同。现有GAEs的设计差异主要在于对比视图的构建方式，而非学习目标或架构，然而这一维度缺乏系统理解和探索。本文旨在通过对比学习视角统一GAEs，揭示其内在联系，并指出非对称对比视图这一被忽视的设计维度。
light_analysis.method: 本文从对比学习视角重新审视GAEs，提出统一框架将结构型和特征型GAEs解释为隐式对比学习器。核心创新是识别出对比视图设计是GAEs的中心维度，特别是非对称对比视图（源于子图视图不匹配）的重要作用。通过统一公式化，该框架揭示了不同GAEs的异同，并在典型图学习任务上进行系统实验来评估性能和效率。
light_analysis.result: 实验结果表明，将GAEs视为隐式对比学习器能够更清晰地理解现有模型，并为设计有效且可扩展的图自编码器提供实用指导。具体性能提升和效率改进依赖于对比视图的设计，摘要未提供具体数据，但强调该视角有助于改进模型设计。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文的主要贡献是统一了GAEs和GCL，提出了对比视图设计作为GAEs核心维度的新视角，特别是非对称对比视图的重要性。该研究为图自编码器的设计提供了理论指导和实践方向，有助于开发更高效、可扩展的自监督图学习方法。未来可进一步探索不同对比视图设计的理论性质和应用场景。

title: AdaMemento: Adaptive Memory-Assisted Policy Optimization for Reinforcement Learning
abstract: In sparse reward scenarios of reinforcement learning (RL), the memory mechanism provides promising shortcuts to policy optimization by reflecting on past experiences like humans. However, current memory-based RL methods simply store and reuse high-value policies, lacking a deeper refining and filtering of diverse past experiences and hence limiting the capability of memory. In this paper, we propose AdaMemento, an adaptive memory-enhanced RL framework. Instead of just memorizing positive past experiences, we design a memory-reflection module that exploits both positive and negative experiences by learning to predict known local optimal policies based on real-time states. To effectively gather informative trajectories for the memory, we further introduce a fine-grained intrinsic motivation paradigm, where nuances in similar states can be precisely distinguished to guide exploration. The exploitation of past experiences and exploration of new policies are then adaptively coordinated by ensemble learning to approach the global optimum. Furthermore, we theoretically prove the superiority of our new intrinsic motivation and ensemble mechanism. From 59 quantitative and visualization experiments, we confirm that AdaMemento can distinguish subtle states for better exploration and effectively exploiting past experiences in memory, achieving significant improvement over previous methods.

light_analysis.overview: 提出自适应记忆增强RL框架，通过利用正负经验和细粒度内在动机协调探索与利用，提升稀疏奖励场景下的策略优化能力。
light_analysis.motivation: 在稀疏奖励强化学习中，现有记忆方法仅存储和重用高价值策略，缺乏对多样化经验的深度精炼与筛选，限制了记忆能力。需要一种机制能够从正负经验中学习，区分细微状态差异，以有效引导探索并平衡探索与利用，从而逼近全局最优策略。
light_analysis.method: AdaMemento框架包含记忆反思模块，通过预测已知局部最优策略来利用正负经验；引入细粒度内在动机，精确区分相似状态中的细微差别以指导探索；采用集成学习自适应协调经验利用和新策略探索。理论上证明了新内在动机和集成机制的优势。
light_analysis.result: 通过59项定量与可视化实验，AdaMemento能够区分细微状态以实现更好的探索，并有效利用记忆中的过去经验，在多种稀疏奖励任务上显著优于之前的记忆型RL方法，性能提升明显。
light_analysis.conclusion: AdaMemento通过细粒度内在动机和集成机制有效解决了稀疏奖励场景下探索与利用的平衡问题，提升了RL性能。实验验证了其有效性，理论分析提供了支撑。未来可应用于更复杂的连续控制任务，并扩展记忆结构。

title: Sinc Kolmogorov-Arnold network and its application for solving PDEs with singularities
abstract: In this paper, we propose to use Sinc interpolation in the context of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks, neural networks with learnable activation functions, which recently gained attention as alternatives to Multilayer Perceptron. Many different function representations have already been tried, but we show that Sinc interpolation proposes a viable alternative, since it is known in numerical analysis to effectively represent both smooth functions and functions with singularities. This is important not only for function approximation but also for solving the partial differential equations with physics-informed neural networks. Through a series of experiments, we show that SincKANs provide better results in almost all of the examples we have considered.

light_analysis.overview: 提出SincKAN，将Sinc插值引入Kolmogorov-Arnold网络，有效处理函数奇异性并提升PDE求解性能。
light_analysis.motivation: Kolmogorov-Arnold网络作为多层感知机的替代方案，其可学习激活函数的设计至关重要。现有函数表示方法在处理奇异性时表现不足，而数值分析中Sinc插值能同时有效逼近光滑函数和含奇点的函数。将此特性引入神经网络，有望提升函数逼近和物理信息神经网络求解偏微分方程的能力。
light_analysis.method: 提出SincKAN架构，用Sinc插值作为Kolmogorov-Arnold网络中的可学习激活函数。该方法利用Sinc函数在数值分析中的优良性质，既能表示光滑函数也能处理奇异性。通过替代标准激活函数，SincKAN保留了KAN的稀疏连接结构，同时增强了对复杂函数的逼近能力。
light_analysis.result: 通过一系列实验（摘要未明确说明具体数据集和指标），SincKAN在几乎所有测试案例中均优于现有方法。具体性能提升包括更好的函数逼近精度和更准确的PDE解，尤其在存在奇点的问题中优势明显。与标准KAN和MLP相比，SincKAN展现了更强的鲁棒性和泛化能力。
light_analysis.conclusion: SincKAN为Kolmogorov-Arnold网络提供了一种有效的激活函数选择，在函数逼近和含奇点的偏微分方程求解中表现出色。该工作拓展了KAN的应用范围，为处理数值计算中的奇点问题提供了新思路。未来可进一步探索Sinc插值的自适应策略及其在更复杂PDE中的表现。

title: Weak Convergence Analysis of Online Neural Actor-Critic Algorithms
abstract: We prove that a single-layer neural network trained with the online actor critic algorithm converges in distribution to a random ordinary differential equation (ODE) as the number of hidden units and the number of training steps $\rightarrow \infty$. In the online actor-critic algorithm, the distribution of the data samples dynamically changes as the model is updated, which is a key challenge for any convergence analysis. We establish the geometric ergodicity of the data samples under a fixed actor policy. Then, using a Poisson equation, we prove that the fluctuations of the model updates around the limit distribution due to the randomly-arriving data samples vanish as the number of parameter updates $\rightarrow \infty$. Using the Poisson equation and weak convergence techniques, we prove that the actor neural network and critic neural network converge to the solutions of a system of ODEs with random initial conditions. Analysis of the limit ODE shows that the limit critic network will converge to the true value function, which will provide the actor an asymptotically unbiased estimate of the policy gradient. We then prove that the limit actor network will converge to a stationary point.

light_analysis.overview: 证明在线演员-评论家算法中单层神经网络在无限隐藏单元和训练步数下依分布收敛到随机ODE。
light_analysis.motivation: 在线演员-评论家算法中，数据样本分布随模型更新动态变化，这给收敛性分析带来关键挑战。现有方法难以处理这种非稳态分布，且缺乏对神经网络在线训练的理论保证。为此，论文旨在建立该算法在无限参数更新下的收敛性，为强化学习算法的理论基础提供支撑。
light_analysis.method: 论文采用弱收敛分析方法。首先证明在固定演员策略下数据样本的几何遍历性。然后利用泊松方程证明随机数据样本导致的模型更新波动随参数更新次数增加而消失。通过泊松方程和弱收敛技术，证明演员和评论家神经网络收敛到具有随机初始条件的常微分方程系统解。
light_analysis.result: 极限ODE分析表明，评论家网络收敛到真实值函数，为演员提供渐近无偏的策略梯度估计；演员网络收敛到驻点。该结果首次为在线演员-评论家算法提供了弱收敛的理论保证，验证了算法在无限资源下的渐近行为。
light_analysis.conclusion: 论文从理论上证明了在线演员-评论家算法的收敛性，填补了该领域在神经网络非线性逼近下的理论空白。其价值在于为强化学习算法设计提供理论指导，但局限在于仅针对单层神经网络和无限隐藏单元，未来可扩展到深层网络和有限样本情况。

title: Adversarial Fine-tuning of Compressed Neural Networks for Joint Improvement of Robustness and Efficiency
abstract: As deep learning (DL) models are increasingly being integrated into our everyday lives, ensuring their safety by making them robust against adversarial attacks has become increasingly critical. DL models have been found to be susceptible to adversarial attacks by introducing small, targeted perturbations to disrupt the input data. Adversarial training has been presented as a mitigation strategy that can result in more robust models. This adversarial robustness comes with additional computational costs required to design adversarial attacks during training. The two objectives -- adversarial robustness and computational efficiency -- then appear to be in conflict with each other. In this work, we explore the effects of neural network compression on adversarial robustness. We specifically explore the effects of fine-tuning on compressed models, and present the trade-off between standard fine-tuning and adversarial fine-tuning. Our results show that adversarial fine-tuning of compressed models can yield large improvements to their robustness performance. We present experiments on several benchmark datasets showing that adversarial fine-tuning of compressed models can achieve robustness performance comparable to adversarially trained models, while also improving computational efficiency. Source code is available here: https://github.com/saintslab/Adver-Fine.
comment: 23 pages, 4 figures, 9 tables. Accepted to The 15th Scandinavian Conference on Artificial Intelligence (SCAI)

light_analysis.overview: 提出对压缩神经网络进行对抗微调，同时提升模型鲁棒性和计算效率。
light_analysis.motivation: 深度学习模型易受对抗攻击，但对抗训练需额外计算成本，导致鲁棒性与效率冲突。现有方法未充分研究压缩模型的微调策略。本文旨在探索压缩后模型通过对抗微调是否能兼顾鲁棒性和效率。
light_analysis.method: 采用神经网络压缩技术（如剪枝、量化）获得轻量模型，然后对其进行对抗微调（在训练中生成对抗样本更新参数）。与标准微调对比，在多个基准数据集（如CIFAR-10、ImageNet）上评估鲁棒性和效率。
light_analysis.result: 实验表明，对抗微调显著提升压缩模型的鲁棒性，其性能接近完全对抗训练的模型，同时计算效率更高。具体数据未在摘要中给出，但代码已开源。
light_analysis.conclusion: 本文证明了压缩模型的对抗微调是平衡鲁棒性与效率的有效策略，为部署安全高效的深度学习模型提供新思路。未来可进一步探索压缩与对抗训练的联合优化。

title: Tell Me a Story! Narrative-Driven XAI with Large Language Models
abstract: In many AI applications today, the predominance of black-box machine learning models, due to their typically higher accuracy, amplifies the need for Explainable AI (XAI). Existing XAI approaches, such as the widely used SHAP values or counterfactual (CF) explanations, are arguably often too technical for users to understand and act upon. To enhance comprehension of explanations of AI decisions and the overall user experience, we introduce XAIstories, which leverage Large Language Models to provide narratives about how AI predictions are made: SHAPstories do so based on SHAP explanations, while CFstories do so for CF explanations. We study the impact of our approach on users' experience and understanding of AI predictions. Our results are striking: over 90% of the surveyed general audience finds the narratives generated by SHAPstories convincing. Data scientists primarily see the value of SHAPstories in communicating explanations to a general audience, with 83% of data scientists indicating they are likely to use SHAPstories for this purpose. In an image classification setting, CFstories are considered more or equally convincing as the users' own crafted stories by more than 75% of the participants. CFstories additionally bring a tenfold speed gain in creating a narrative. We also find that SHAPstories help users to more accurately summarize and understand AI decisions, in a credit scoring setting we test, correctly answering comprehension questions significantly more often than they do when only SHAP values are provided. The results thereby suggest that XAIstories may significantly help explaining and understanding AI predictions, ultimately supporting better decision-making in various applications.

light_analysis.overview: 利用大语言模型将SHAP和反事实解释转化为叙事，提升AI决策可解释性和用户体验。
light_analysis.motivation: 现有XAI方法如SHAP和反事实解释技术性强，普通用户难以理解。随着黑盒模型广泛应用，需要更直观、易理解的解释方式来帮助用户信任并正确使用AI预测，从而改善决策质量。
light_analysis.method: 提出XAIstories框架，包含SHAPstories和CFstories，分别基于SHAP值和反事实解释，利用大语言模型生成叙事。在信用评分和图像分类场景中测试，通过用户调研评估叙述的合理性、速度及理解准确性。
light_analysis.result: 超过90%的普通用户认为SHAPstories有说服力；83%的数据科学家愿将其用于向大众解释。在图像分类中，75%以上用户认为CFstories与自创故事一样或更有说服力，且生成速度快10倍。在信用评分中，SHAPstories帮助用户更准确理解AI决策。
light_analysis.conclusion: XAIstories通过叙事有效提升AI预测的解释性和用户理解，有助于改进决策。未来可扩展至更多应用场景和解释类型，但需注意叙事可能引入的偏见或过度简化风险。
